Package io.forja.components.dataDisplay.fxAvatarGroup

Class FxAvatarGroup

java.lang.Object
javafx.scene.Node
javafx.scene.Parent
javafx.scene.layout.Region
javafx.scene.layout.Pane
javafx.scene.layout.HBox
io.forja.components.dataDisplay.fxAvatarGroup.FxAvatarGroup
All Implemented Interfaces:
javafx.css.Styleable, javafx.event.EventTarget
public class FxAvatarGroup extends javafx.scene.layout.HBox
A horizontal stack of overlapping FxAvatars with an optional overflow count.

FxAvatarGroup extends HBox and renders the first getMax() avatars from getAvatars(), applying a negative left margin so they overlap. When there are more avatars than max, a final "+N" overflow badge is appended.

The preferred way to construct an FxAvatarGroup is via the builder:

     
      FxAvatarGroup team = FxAvatarGroup.builder()
          .avatar("MB")
          .avatar("AK")
          .avatar("RS")
          .avatar("JD")
          .avatar("LP")
          .max(3)
          .size(AvatarSize.DEFAULT)
          .build();
See Also:
  • FxAvatar
  • AvatarSize
  • FxAvatarGroup.Builder

  • Method Details

    • getAvatars

      public javafx.collections.ObservableList<FxAvatar> getAvatars()
      Returns the underlying mutable avatar list. Mutating this list triggers a rebuild.

      public AvatarSize getSize()

    • setSize

      public void setSize(AvatarSize v)
      Sets the size variant — propagates to every member avatar.

    • getOverflowBadge

      public FxAvatar getOverflowBadge()
      Returns the current overflow badge node, or null when no overflow is shown. Useful for tests and advanced styling.

    • builder

      public static FxAvatarGroup.Builder builder()
      Returns a new FxAvatarGroup.Builder for constructing an FxAvatarGroup.
      Returns:
      a new FxAvatarGroup.Builder instance
